original_hypothesis.title: Unauthorized reserve drain if drawManager is misconfigured or unset (address(0)) at deployment, enabling hostile takeover via setDrawManager
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PrizePool.setDrawManager is callable by anyone until drawManager is set. If the PrizePool is deployed with drawManager = address(0) (or reset via redeploy), an attacker can set themselves as drawManager and then call withdrawReserve to drain the reserve and control closeDraw (including choosing winningRandomNumber). Although the chain has no public mempool, this remains a realistic configuration/ops hazard if deployment is not atomic or if initial drawManager is intentionally left unset.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a high-impact configuration footgun explicitly noted in code and Phase A invariants. Validation should focus on whether production deployments ever leave drawManager unset and whether deployment tooling enforces immediate initialization.
deployment_assumptions: PrizePool constructor param drawManager is set to address(0), and no immediate atomic transaction sets it correctly
Or the intended drawManager transaction can be delayed/failed (ops incident)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PrizePool.sol constructor allows params.drawManager == address(0); setDrawManager has no auth until set (PrizePool.sol setDrawManager). No deployment scripts provided that guarantee non-zero drawManager.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setDrawManager: if drawManager == address(0) anyone can set; onlyDrawManager gates withdrawReserve/closeDraw

preconditions: drawManager == address(0) on a live PrizePool with funds
Attacker can submit transactions (no ordering assumptions needed)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: One-time unauthenticated initialization of a privileged role controlling reserve withdrawal.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: From deployment until drawManager is set (or any redeploy with unset manager).

operational_questions: Do deployment procedures ever set drawManager later instead of in constructor?
Are there automated checks that reject drawManager == 0 deployments?
Is reserve ever funded before drawManager is confirmed set?
validation_requirements: Review deployment pipelines (scripts/CI) for drawManager initialization
On-chain verify historical deployments: drawManager set in constructor vs later tx
Simulate hostile setDrawManager on a test deployment with drawManager unset

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Optionally manipulate closeDraw to bias outcomes

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Full control of draw lifecycle and reserve withdrawals
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Loss of reserve, compromised randomness, broken trust
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All prize distribution and reserve management

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
